rationale: Verified the only production change (internal/setup/claude.go) is a pure append to the embedded lifecycleSkillFiles() literal — 15 lines inserted after the existing session-close protocol block, before the closing backtick/brace; nothing removed or reordered. `git diff --name-status 2f45d8d9..1733886e` shows internal/lifecycle/ownership_test.go is new (A), and no existing test file was touched (only claude.go, 2 skill docs, and the ADR are M). Ran `go test -count=1 ./internal/setup ./cmd/mindspec ./internal/lifecycle` — all green. `gofmt -l ./cmd ./internal` empty. Checked the two existing tests that inspect the ms-impl-approve literal (skills_test.go skill-existence list, claude_test.go TestLifecycleSkills_CanonicalApproveOrder which regex-scans for deprecated `approve impl`/`mindspec approve` phrasing and requires the canonical `mindspec impl approve <id>` string): neither asserts exact byte-length, and the appended text only uses canonical `mindspec impl approve` / `mindspec complete` phrasing, so it cannot trip the deprecated-order regex. No regression in setup/skill materialization; skill-file count and canonical-order invariants hold.
